journals.content: Saturday night and I finally beat that boss in Elden Ring after like 20 tries. Felt good to have control for once. But now my brain's replaying that awkward chat with my roommate earlier—did I say something dumb?

journals.title: Sunday Scroll
journals.content: Woke up checking emails—nothing urgent, thank god. Planning to customize my keyboard today, got those new switches ordered. Maybe it'll help with the typing anxiety during calls.
First day back to work blues hit hard. Boss mentioned a team meeting tomorrow and I'm already spiraling about what if I freeze up? Lunch was that safe pad thai delivery.

journals.title: Meeting Aftermath
journals.content: The meeting wasn't as bad as I thought, but I stuttered on my update. Still thinking about it. Swiped on Tinder tonight—matched with someone, but waiting for a reply is killing me. Typos in my bio probably scared her off.
Midday break. Tried cooking that chicken recipe—followed it to the letter, turned out okay. But 'add salt to taste' made me sweat; ended up googling it three times.

journals.title: App Anxiety
journals.content: She texted back! We chatted a bit, but now I'm overthinking every response. Work project is piling up—deadline Friday, and I hate open-ended tasks. Need to game later to unwind.
Friday finally. Finished most of the code, boss liked it in Slack. But the match ghosted me after I suggested coffee—knew the unknowns would bite. Keyboard build almost done, feels satisfying.

journals.title: Weekend Start
journals.content: No work alarm! Slept in a bit. Gonna binge that show tonight—safe reruns help with the sleep transition. Reflecting on the week: progress on hobbies, but social stuff still sucks.

journals.title: Gratitude Moment
journals.content: Sunday evening. Cooked pancakes from a precise recipe—didn't burn them! Grateful for remote work keeping the chaos low. Maybe try dating app again tomorrow, but smaller steps.

journals.title: Monday Motivation?
journals.content: Back to the grind. Project update went smooth, feeling a bit more confident. But that loop of worst-case scenarios about the weekend's nothing plans is starting already. Push through.
Work was okay today, but that standup meeting had me sweating over my update. Stuttered a bit, but no one seemed to notice. Still replaying it in my head though.

journals.title: Morning dread
journals.content: Woke up to an email from boss about deadline—heart raced. Why can't things just be predictable? Gonna chug coffee and dive into code.
Beat that boss in Elden Ring finally. Felt like a win after the ghosting fiasco last week. Maybe I can handle real life too? Nah, probably not.

journals.title: Lunch thoughts
journals.content: Ordered pad thai again—recipe in app is exact, no surprises. Thinking about swiping on Tinder but what if another ghost? Ugh.
Keyboard clicks are so satisfying after a crap day. Teammate's Slack joke went over my head, felt dumb. Weekend can't come soon enough.

journals.title: Saturday unwind
journals.content: Cooked that chicken recipe to the letter—turned out perfect. No disasters. Watched old episodes, but mind keeps wandering to dating apps. Should I message someone?

journals.title: Sunday blues early
journals.content: Slept okay but dreaming about work meetings. Gonna game all day to forget. Update on keyboard: added blue switches, love it.

journals.title: Small victory
journals.content: Boss praised my code in chat! First time in weeks. Heart still pounding from nerves, but yay? Might try yoga app for anxiety.
Swiped right on a girl who likes RPGs. Matched! But now waiting for reply is killing me. Don't overthink, Alex.

journals.title: Late night reflection
journals.content: She texted back—casual chat going okay so far. Not ghosted yet! Work week's ahead, but feeling a tiny bit hopeful. Grateful for routines keeping me sane.
Woke up to an email from boss about next week's sprint. Heart racing already— what if I mess up the demo? Need to plan every slide today.

journals.title: Valentine's Blues
journals.content: Single on V-day sucks. That new match, Sarah, messaged about our fave RPGs last night—Elden Ring bosses are brutal but fun. Replied but now overthinking if it was witty enough. Ordered pizza, no cooking tonight.
Lunch delivered, ate while staring at code. Sarah asked if I'd play co-op sometime. Panic mode: what if I'm bad at it? Said maybe, now regretting.

journals.title: Late Night Loop
journals.content: Still thinking about that standup comment last week—did boss mean I'm slacking? Scrolled Reddit till eyes hurt. Tomorrow's Monday, ugh. Bedtime routine failed again.

journals.title: Work Win?
journals.content: Fixed a bug that's been haunting the project. Boss slack'd 'nice catch!' Feels good, but waiting for more feedback is killing me. Gaming later to unwind—need that save point comfort.
Morning check: Sarah suggested a virtual hangout this weekend. Excited but terrified—voice chat? What do I say? Deep breaths, Alex.

journals.title: Keyboard Therapy
journals.content: Tinkered with my mechanical keyboard setup—new switches feel clicky perfect. Distracted from deadline stress. Update on Sarah: we chatted more, she's cool. Hope I don't screw it up.

journals.title: Boring Thursday
journals.content: Routine day. Cooked that precise stir-fry recipe, no disasters. Work's okay, no meetings. Just existing.

journals.title: Weekend Vibes
journals.content: Friday finally! Beat a tough Elden Ring boss after 20 tries—small victory. Sarah confirmed tomorrow's voice chat. Heart pounding, but maybe it'll be fun? Fingers crossed.

journals.title: Chat Aftermath
journals.content: Talked to Sarah for an hour—laughed about game glitches. No awkward silences like I feared! Feeling hopeful, though now replaying every word in my head. Progress?
Woke up to another email from boss about the sprint demo tomorrow. Heart racing already. Just gonna stick to my code and hope no questions come my way.

journals.title: Demo Day
journals.content: The demo went okay? Boss said 'good job' but I keep replaying that awkward pause when I explained the bug fix. Sarah texted back finally, wants to chat about Elden Ring bosses tonight. Kinda excited but what if I say something dumb?
Lunch ordered, same Thai place. Still thinking about Sarah's laugh on the call. Maybe I should suggest a virtual date? No, too soon.

journals.title: Late Night Thoughts
journals.content: Chatted with Sarah for an hour again. She's funny, shares my hate for vague quests in games. But now I'm overthinking if she really likes me or just being nice. Can't sleep.
Work's piling up with new tickets. Fixed that login glitch though, felt good. Keyboard clicks are soothing me through this.

journals.title: Friday Relief
journals.content: Weekend! Cooked that exact pasta recipe, no disasters. Sarah asked if I'd play co-op sometime. Co-op? In Elden Ring? Terrifying but yeah, I said maybe. Heart pounding.
Sunday gaming binge. Leveled up my character, total control. Wish real life had save points. Update on Sarah: she sent a meme, laughed out loud.

journals.title: Monday Blues
journals.content: Back to emails. Boss wants status update meeting. Why can't it just be async? Feel like hiding under desk.

journals.title: Progress?
journals.content: Meeting wasn't bad, got praise on the sprint. Sarah and I scheduled a voice call for tomorrow - and she mentioned coffee sometime? IRL? Panicking but trying to stay calm. Tinkered with keyboard switches instead of freaking out.

Call with Sarah was great, talked recipes too - she likes precise ones like me. But now brain's looping on coffee idea. What if I spill something? Or worse, awkward silence? Need sleep but re-watching old show.
Woke up to Sarah's message replaying in my head. She suggested coffee this weekend—am I ready for that? Heart racing just thinking about it.

journals.title: Lunch Blues
journals.content: Ordered pad thai again, left at door. Boss's email about the demo tomorrow has me spiraling—what if I freeze up on the call?
Demo went okay, I think? Got a 'good job' in Slack. Still overthinking that pause when I explained the bug fix. Sarah texted back—said she's free Saturday. Oh god.

journals.title: Late Night Scroll
journals.content: Couldn't sleep, watched old episodes of that show. Updated my keyboard switches today, feels smoother. But Sarah... what if conversation dies at coffee?
Monday already? Weekend plans looming. Messaged Sarah yes to coffee. Now panicking about outfit and small talk.

journals.title: Evening Reflection
journals.content: Work was a grind, deadline pushed back thank god. Cooked that chicken recipe—measured everything perfectly. Still buzzing about tomorrow's meetup with Sarah.

journals.title: Post-Coffee Jitters
journals.content: Met Sarah! It was... nice? We talked games for an hour, laughed about Elden Ring fails. Didn't screw up too bad. Heart still pounding though.
Replaying coffee date in my head—did she seem bored? Work bug hunt was therapeutic today.

journals.title: Can't Sleep
journals.content: Sarah suggested gaming together online this week. Excited but terrified I'll say something dumb. Keyboard typing feels great at least.

journals.title: Gratitude Moment
journals.content: Friday! Beat that tough boss in the game. Positive feedback on code review. Sarah's meme made me smile—things might be okay.
Gamed with Sarah—co-op was fun, no major awkwardness. Exhausted but hopeful. What's next?

Weekend winding down, but my brain won't shut off. Still replaying that coffee date with Sarah—did I say something dumb? Played some RPG after, felt safe in the game world. Need to text her tomorrow, ugh.

journals.title: Monday dread
journals.content: Woke up to a Slack ping from boss about weekly sync. Why can't everything be async? Texted Sarah good morning, she replied quick—heart racing but kinda nice.
Lunch delivered, ate while staring at code. Meeting went okay, no disasters. Sarah suggested co-op gaming tonight—nervous but excited? Fingers crossed.

journals.title: Game night update
journals.content: Gaming with Sarah was fun! We laughed at my character's dumb choices. She's inviting me to a low-key dinner this weekend. Is this real?
Work dragged, decision on project direction stressing me out. Ordered pizza again. Thinking about dinner with Sarah— what if I burn something?

journals.title: Recipe hunt
journals.content: Found a precise recipe for chicken stir-fry, gonna practice for Saturday. Work email praised my last commit—small win. Sarah's meme made me smile.
Friday finally. Boss threw a curveball task, but I handled it solo. Texting Sarah about tomorrow—hope I don't overthink the menu.

journals.title: Pre-date jitters
journals.content: Keyboard tinkering to calm nerves. Dinner tomorrow at my place? She said yes. What if conversation dies? But her voice call earlier was warm.

journals.title: Date recap
journals.content: Dinner with Sarah went better than expected! Stir-fry wasn't perfect but she ate it. We talked for hours, no awkward silences. Feeling hopeful.
Lazy Sunday gaming solo. Still buzzing from yesterday—Sarah wants to do this again. Work off, but mind racing about next week.

journals.title: Back to grind
journals.content: Monday blues hit hard, deadline looming. Gratitude for Sarah though, her text cheered me up. Might try a new recipe soon.
Long day, fixed a bug that was haunting me. Sarah and I planned a movie night—virtual for now. Sleep calling, but brain won't quit.
